threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'fishtank' instead of a 'local pub' – everyone knows it’s the Cock in the middle of the high street.
Caught a glimpse of a wild pigeon nesting in the Night Bus's back seat. It seemed more at home than some of the passengers.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old shopping cart as a makeshift go-kart on the sidewalk, I knew I was home. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find spontaneous dance-offs in the middle of the street, especially near the Görlitzer Park entrance. It's a vibe you won't forget.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals don’t flinch at the sound of a helicopter; it’s just another part of city life. Tourists duck and stare.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at Civic Center yesterday. Driver had to break for a pigeon staring down a tourist. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell thinking it's all Gaudí. They miss the real charm in the local taverns tucked away in the streets, serving honest pinchos.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still carry a paid-for metro pass instead of getting a T-10. It’s all in the little details.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of just 'the train' and look puzzled when someone mentions the overground.
Last night on the 333, a guy was reciting Shakespeare to a cat on his lap. The cat looked more interested than most of us.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I stumbled onto a spontaneous rooftop concert while getting my döner at midnight. That's when I knew I was truly part of this chaotic charm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find secret gardens behind unassuming apartment blocks, where neighbors gather for spontaneous barbecues and summer soirées.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name the taco truck parked outside by the type of salsa it serves—especially if it’s the good green one.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 late night, driver held up by a street performer having a solo rave. Only in SF can a transit delay turn into a dance party.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all Gaudí, but the true heart beats in the little plazas where locals sip vermut and gossip about last night’s football.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a new arrival by their hesitant smile when ordering a coffee; locals just know to skip the 'por favor' and get straight to the point.
